title: Novartis — Projects
html:
Novartis Active Projects & Workstreams

Governing Thought

The Novartis project portfolio spans 24 engagements across 7 years and 12+ brands — the broadest therapeutic coverage of any ZoomRx account. Three structural patterns define this portfolio: (1) HCP-Patient Conversations is the overwhelming dominant methodology, used on 9+ brands, making it ZoomRx's signature offering at Novartis; (2) the Rhapsido PET (Wave 5) is the only recurring tracker, creating a single-point revenue dependency; and (3) most brands were engaged once with a single HCP-Pt study and never followed up with additional services, leaving significant upsell potential unrealized. The active project count has stabilized at 3-4, down from the peak activity of 2023-2024 when 6-8 projects ran simultaneously — a signal that account growth requires deliberate reactivation of dormant brand relationships.

Account Health Dashboard

Active Projects

Project Brand Service Status Last Activity Key Detail
Rhapsido PET Wave 5 Remibrutinib/Rhapsido PET :green_circle: Active Apr 2026 Full Report v1 (Apr 17, 2026); deepest recurring engagement
Abelacimab HCP-Pt + MI Abelacimab HCP-Pt + Market Intelligence :green_circle: Active Jul 2025 (KO) KO, Market Intelligence, Survey Draft; pipeline brand
PSP Study Proposal [TBD] Proposal :blue_circle: Proposal Apr 2026 Feasibility Estimates, Proposal Decks
SAGAN Answers Demo Cross-brand Data Integration Demo :yellow_circle: Strategic Apr 2026 Contains Rhapsido PET W5 report + Remibrutinib raw data

Key Completed Projects (Sorted by Recency)

# Project Brand Service Last Activity Key Detail
15 mHSPC HCP-Pt Prostate cancer (mHSPC) HCP-Pt Apr 2025 Metastatic hormone-sensitive prostate cancer
14 Pluvicto PET Pluvicto PET Mar 2025 Promotional tracking for radioligand therapy
13 CSU Remibrutinib HCP-Pt Remibrutinib/CSU HCP-Pt Mar 2025 Chronic spontaneous urticaria conversations
12 CSU PET + Digital Tracking Remibrutinib/CSU PET + DT Dec 2024 Combined PET and Digital Tracking
-- Xolair Benchmarks Data Xolair Benchmarks Sep 2024 Competitive benchmarking data
11 Pluvicto HCP-Pt Pluvicto HCP-Pt Sep 2024 Radioligand therapy patient conversations
10 Kisqali HCP-Pt Kisqali HCP-Pt Feb 2024 Breast cancer CDK4/6 conversations
09 PNH HCP-Pt (Fabhalta) Fabhalta/Iptacopan HCP-Pt Jan 2024 Oral PNH therapy conversations
-- Zolgensma Caregiver + HCP Qual Zolgensma Qual 2023 SMA gene therapy dual qual
08 ITP/wAIHA/PNH HCP-Pt Multi-indication HCP-Pt Nov 2023 Multi-indication hematology study
07 Scemblix Zero-Cost PET Scemblix PET (zero-cost) Oct 2023 CML proof-of-concept PET
06 Scemblix HCP-Pt Scemblix HCP-Pt Oct 2023 CML patient conversations
05 Pelacarsen HCP-Pt Pelacarsen HCP-Pt Oct 2023 CV pipeline Lp(a) conversations
04 Leqvio HCP-Pt Leqvio HCP-Pt Oct 2023 CV siRNA therapy conversations
03 Crizanlizumab DSA PET Crizanlizumab PET 2019 Sickle cell disease; first Novartis project
02 Promacta Promacta [TBD] Pre-2023 ITP hematology; early-era project
01 Tasigna Tasigna [TBD] Pre-2023 CML; early-era project

Legend: :green_circle: Active | :blue_circle: Proposal/New | :yellow_circle: Strategic/Watch | :white_circle: Completed

Detailed Workstream Profiles

Rhapsido (Remibrutinib) — CSU Tracking Workstream

Rhapsido PET — Wave 5

Status: Active | Latest: Full Report v1 (Apr 17, 2026) | Service: PET

What it is: The deepest and most mature tracking engagement at Novartis. Promotional Effectiveness Tracking for remibrutinib (trade name Rhapsido) across allergists and dermatologists in the chronic spontaneous urticaria (CSU) market.

Strategic significance:

  • Wave 5 provides 5 waves of longitudinal trend data — no other Novartis brand has this depth
  • Dual specialist tracking (allergists + dermatologists) captures how messages land differently across prescriber types
  • The primary competitive benchmark is Xolair (omalizumab)
  • This is the only recurring revenue stream on the Novartis account
  • Data from this PET was included in the SAGAN Answers demo — signaling Novartis's interest in integrating PET data into internal AI analytics

Watch items:

  • SAGAN integration may change how this data is consumed — monitor whether Novartis begins requesting raw data vs. analyzed reports
  • Wave 6+ continuation should be confirmed proactively
  • Upsell opportunity: add ATU and/or message testing to the Rhapsido workstream

Contact: [Populate — CSU brand insights lead]

Remibrutinib CSU HCP-Pt

Status: Completed | Latest: Mar 2025

HCP-Patient Conversations study for remibrutinib in CSU. Complements the PET by capturing qualitative exam-room dynamics — how physicians discuss a BTK inhibitor for CSU with patients, competitive positioning vs. Xolair, and patient concerns.

CSU PET + Digital Tracking

Status: Completed | Latest: Dec 2024

Combined PET and Digital Tracking study for remibrutinib in CSU. The digital tracking component monitored online patient behavior related to CSU treatment.

Oncology Workstream

Pluvicto HCP-Pt + PET

Status: Completed | Latest: Mar 2025 (PET), Sep 2024 (HCP-Pt)

Multi-study engagement: Two HCP-Pt studies (2024, 2025) and one PET study (2025) for Pluvicto, Novartis's radioligand therapy for mCRPC.

Why it matters:

  • Radioligand therapy is a novel modality requiring fundamentally different patient communication than traditional oncology agents
  • The multi-study depth (2 HCP-Pt + 1 PET) makes Pluvicto one of the most-studied brands on the account
  • No active follow-up project — this engagement is dormant despite being the strongest oncology proof point

Follow-up opportunities:

  • MSL/MEE Tracking — radioligand therapy requires extensive MSL scientific education; ZoomRx's MSL methodology is a natural fit
  • Continued PET tracking — promotional effectiveness tracking for a growing >$1.5B brand
  • SPLASH trial data readout — if earlier-line data is positive, new patient populations create new research needs

Contact: [Populate — GU Oncology Insights Lead]

mHSPC HCP-Pt

Status: Completed | Latest: Apr 2025

HCP-Patient Conversations for metastatic hormone-sensitive prostate cancer. This engagement may involve Pluvicto or other Novartis agents in the mHSPC space — the competitive landscape includes enzalutamide, apalutamide, darolutamide, and abiraterone.

Kisqali HCP-Pt

Status: Completed | Latest: Feb 2024

Strategic significance: Kisqali is Novartis's largest oncology brand (~$4B+ [UNVERIFIED]) with a single completed HCP-Pt study and no active follow-up. This is arguably the highest-value reactivation target on the entire account — a brand of this scale running no active promotional tracking represents a major white-space opportunity.

Follow-up opportunities:

  • PET — Kisqali has no active promotional effectiveness tracking despite being a multi-billion-dollar brand in a competitive three-way CDK4/6 race
  • ATU — Awareness/trial/usage tracking in the evolving adjuvant and metastatic breast cancer settings
  • MSL Tracking — NATALEE trial data dissemination requires MSL engagement

Contact: [Populate — Breast Cancer Insights Lead]

Scemblix Zero-Cost PET + HCP-Pt

Status: Completed | Latest: Oct 2023

Two studies on Scemblix (asciminib) for CML: a zero-cost PET (proof-of-concept) and an HCP-Pt study. The zero-cost PET suggests the brand team was testing ZoomRx's PET capabilities — conversion to a paid PET engagement was the expected follow-up but has not materialized (2+ years dormant).

Watch items:

  • 2+ years with no follow-up suggests either the brand team moved on, budget constraints intervened, or the zero-cost PET did not compel paid conversion
  • CML is a smaller market than breast cancer or prostate cancer — lower revenue ceiling per study

Hematology Workstream

PNH HCP-Pt (Fabhalta/Iptacopan)

Status: Completed | Latest: Jan 2024

HCP-Patient Conversations for Fabhalta (iptacopan), the first oral complement factor B inhibitor for PNH. The oral administration differentiator vs. IV/SubQ competitors (Soliris/Ultomiris) was the central messaging theme.

Follow-up opportunities:

  • PET — Fabhalta is a launch-phase brand with no active promotional tracking
  • Label expansion studies — C3G and IgAN label expansions could generate new HCP-Pt or ATU work

ITP/wAIHA/PNH Multi-Indication HCP-Pt

Status: Completed | Latest: Nov 2023

Multi-indication study covering ITP, warm autoimmune hemolytic anemia, and PNH. This is a distinctive study design — running a single HCP-Pt engagement across three related hematologic conditions — that demonstrated ZoomRx's ability to deliver cross-condition insights efficiently.

Cardiovascular Workstream

Leqvio HCP-Pt

Status: Completed | Latest: Oct 2023

HCP-Patient Conversations for Leqvio (inclisiran), the twice-yearly siRNA for LDL-C reduction. The unique dosing model (2x/year injection) creates distinctive patient conversations compared to daily/monthly alternatives.

Follow-up opportunities:

  • PET — competitive CV market with Repatha and Praluent
  • ATU — awareness/trial/usage for a launch-phase CV specialty agent

Pelacarsen HCP-Pt

Status: Completed | Latest: Oct 2023

HCP-Patient Conversations for Pelacarsen, the pipeline Lp(a)-lowering antisense oligonucleotide. This pre-launch HCP-Pt study positions ZoomRx for launch research if Pelacarsen is approved.

Pipeline Workstream

Abelacimab HCP-Pt + Market Intelligence

Status: Active | Latest: Jul 2025 (KO) | Service: HCP-Pt + MI

The most recent new-brand entry at Novartis. Abelacimab is an anti-Factor XI monoclonal antibody for anticoagulation — a novel approach that decouples antithrombotic effect from bleeding risk.

Strategic significance:

  • Active project with survey draft and market intelligence components
  • Pipeline brand with launch potential — positions ZoomRx for launch tracking
  • Novel mechanism creates patient communication challenges similar to Pluvicto (new modality education)

Contact: [Populate]

PSP Study Proposal

Status: Proposal | Latest: Apr 2026

Active proposal with feasibility estimates and proposal decks. The therapeutic area and brand are not specified in the SharePoint data.

Action items:

  • Identify the brand and TA for this proposal
  • Confirm proposal status and decision timeline
  • Estimate revenue if won

SAGAN Answers Demo

Status: Strategic/Watch | Latest: Apr 2026

Not a traditional project — this is a data integration demonstration where ZoomRx data (Rhapsido PET Wave 5 Full Report and Remibrutinib raw data) is being evaluated for ingestion into Novartis's internal SAGAN AI analytics platform.

Strategic significance:

  • This could evolve into a new engagement model where ZoomRx provides structured data feeds optimized for SAGAN consumption
  • Alternatively, it could commoditize ZoomRx's role if Novartis uses SAGAN to replace ZoomRx's analytical layer
  • The outcome of this demo will shape the account's trajectory

Action items:

  • Clarify who initiated this demo (Novartis pull vs. ZoomRx push)
  • Understand what SAGAN does with the ingested data
  • Position ZoomRx as the analytical intelligence layer, not just the data source

Cross-Workstream Themes

1. HCP-Pt Is the Franchise — But It Has Not Generated Recurring Revenue

HCP-Patient Conversations has been deployed on 9+ brands — a remarkable breadth of adoption. But each engagement was a single study with no follow-up. The methodology creates trust and demonstrates capability, yet the account team has not systematically converted HCP-Pt studies into PET, ATU, or other recurring services. The sole exception is Rhapsido, where HCP-Pt was followed by PET and Digital Tracking.

Implication: The historical HCP-Pt studies are an asset — they are 9 warm leads for upselling. The priority should be systematically reactivating the highest-value brand relationships (Kisqali, Pluvicto, Fabhalta) with specific PET or ATU proposals anchored in the original HCP-Pt findings.

2. The 2023 Surge Did Not Sustain

2023 was the peak year with 6+ simultaneous projects across hematology, CV, oncology, and gene therapy. But many of those brand engagements were not renewed or expanded. The project cadence has slowed to 3-4 active projects by 2026.

Implication: The account needs a deliberate reactivation strategy. The 2023 project volume proves demand exists across Novartis TAs — the question is why it was not sustained and what relationship or budget dynamics caused the slowdown.

3. Oncology Is the Largest Untapped Revenue Pool

Novartis's oncology portfolio (Kisqali ~$4B+, Pluvicto ~$1.5B, Scemblix) has been engaged through one-time HCP-Pt and PET studies but has no active tracking or recurring work. Given the competitive intensity (CDK4/6 race, radioligand therapy adoption, CML treatment landscape), oncology should be the primary target for service expansion.

4. SAGAN Is the Account's Strategic Fork

The SAGAN Answers demo is not a project in the traditional sense — it is a signal about the future shape of the relationship. The two paths are:

  • Path A (Partner): ZoomRx becomes an embedded analytics partner within the SAGAN ecosystem, providing AI-enriched insights that SAGAN surfaces to decision-makers. Revenue grows through integration premium.
  • Path B (Utility): ZoomRx becomes a data supplier feeding raw data into SAGAN, which Novartis uses for internal analytics. Revenue is maintained but margins compress and ZoomRx becomes substitutable.

Implication: Engage with the SAGAN team proactively. Shape the integration model before it is decided for ZoomRx.

Immediate Priorities

  1. Confirm Rhapsido PET Wave 6 continuation. The recurring revenue anchor must be secured before pursuing growth. Proactively propose Wave 6+ and explore adding ATU or message testing to the Rhapsido workstream.

  2. Reactivate Kisqali brand relationship. A >$4B brand with a single completed HCP-Pt and no active work is the highest-value opportunity on the account. Prepare a PET proposal tailored to the CDK4/6 competitive landscape.

  3. Clarify PSP Study Proposal status. Identify the brand, TA, and decision timeline. This is the most immediate near-term revenue opportunity.

  4. Engage on SAGAN strategically. Understand who initiated the demo, what Novartis intends to do with ZoomRx data in SAGAN, and how to position ZoomRx as the insights layer (not the data utility).

  5. Identify procurement contact. The vendor evaluation is happening. Being proactive with a portfolio case study and framework proposal positions ZoomRx favorably.

  6. Pitch Pluvicto MSL/MEE. Radioligand therapy requires extensive MSL scientific education — this is a natural MSL/MEE tracking opportunity that no other service type can address.

Project Timeline (Full History)

# Project Year Service Brand / TA
-- SAGAN Answers Demo Apr 2026 Data Integration Cross-brand
-- PSP Study Proposal Apr 2026 Proposal TBD
-- Rhapsodo PET Wave 5 Apr 2026 PET Remibrutinib/CSU
-- Procurement File Sharing Jan 2026 -- Cross-brand
16 Abelacimab HCP-Pt + MI Jul 2025 HCP-Pt + MI Abelacimab/Anticoag
15 mHSPC HCP-Pt Apr 2025 HCP-Pt Prostate Cancer
14 Pluvicto PET Mar 2025 PET Pluvicto/Prostate
13 Remibrutinib CSU HCP-Pt Mar 2025 HCP-Pt Remibrutinib/CSU
12 CSU PET + Digital Tracking Dec 2024 PET + DT Remibrutinib/CSU
-- Xolair Benchmarks Sep 2024 Benchmarks Xolair/CSU
11 Pluvicto HCP-Pt (2nd) Sep 2024 HCP-Pt Pluvicto/Prostate
10 Kisqali HCP-Pt Feb 2024 HCP-Pt Kisqali/Breast
09 PNH HCP-Pt (Fabhalta) Jan 2024 HCP-Pt Fabhalta/Hematology
-- Zolgensma Caregiver + HCP Qual 2023 Qual Zolgensma/SMA
08 ITP/wAIHA/PNH HCP-Pt Nov 2023 HCP-Pt Multi-indication/Heme
07 Scemblix Zero-Cost PET Oct 2023 PET (zero-cost) Scemblix/CML
06 Scemblix HCP-Pt Oct 2023 HCP-Pt Scemblix/CML
05 Pelacarsen HCP-Pt Oct 2023 HCP-Pt Pelacarsen/CV
04 Leqvio HCP-Pt Oct 2023 HCP-Pt Leqvio/CV
03 Crizanlizumab DSA PET 2019 PET Crizanlizumab/SCD
02 Promacta Pre-2023 TBD Promacta/ITP
01 Tasigna Pre-2023 TBD Tasigna/CML

Back to Accounts Hub | Home

The SAGAN Answers demo is not a project in the traditional sense — it is a signal about the future shape of the relationship. The two paths are:

Path A (Partner): ZoomRx becomes an embedded analytics partner within the SAGAN ecosystem, providing AI-enriched insights that SAGAN surfaces to decision-makers. Revenue grows through integration premium.
Path B (Utility): ZoomRx becomes a data supplier feeding raw data into SAGAN, which Novartis uses for internal analytics. Revenue is maintained but margins compress and ZoomRx becomes substitutable.

Implication: Engage with the SAGAN team proactively. Shape the integration model before it is decided for ZoomRx.

Immediate Priorities


Confirm Rhapsido PET Wave 6 continuation. The recurring revenue anchor must be secured before pursuing growth. Proactively propose Wave 6+ and explore adding ATU or message testing to the Rhapsido workstream.


Reactivate Kisqali brand relationship. A >$4B brand with a single completed HCP-Pt and no active work is the highest-value opportunity on the account. Prepare a PET proposal tailored to the CDK4/6 competitive landscape.


Clarify PSP Study Proposal status. Identify the brand, TA, and decision timeline. This is the most immediate near-term revenue opportunity.


Engage on SAGAN strategically. Understand who initiated the demo, what Novartis intends to do with ZoomRx data in SAGAN, and how to position ZoomRx as the insights layer (not the data utility).


Identify procurement contact. The vendor evaluation is happening. Being proactive with a portfolio case study and framework proposal positions ZoomRx favorably.


Pitch Pluvicto MSL/MEE. Radioligand therapy requires extensive MSL scientific education — this is a natural MSL/MEE tracking opportunity that no other service type can address.
